title: Visual Rule Builder for Creating and Editing Business Rules
description: The system shall provide a visual rule builder for creating and editing business rules using structured dropdown condition rows. Not drag-and-drop, not raw code editor. The interaction pattern shall be: Field → Operator → Value with AND/OR grouping.
recommendedApproach: Dropdown + inline value controls + AND/OR groups + rule testing
evaluatedLibrary: React Query Builder as the underlying interaction model, customized to Group Benefits domain

demoLayout.header: Rule Name + Rule ID + Domain + Status
demoLayout.conditionsSection: IF [ Field ] [ Operator ] [ Value ] [delete] | AND/OR | [ + Add Condition ]
demoLayout.actionsSection: THEN [ Action Field ] [ Action Type ] [ Value ] [delete] | [ + Add Action ]

title: Field → Operator → Value Condition Construction
description: The rule builder shall support Field → Operator → Value condition construction for each condition row.

title: Dynamic Operators and Value Controls Based on Field Data Type
description: The rule builder shall dynamically determine available operators and value control types based on the selected field data type. Selecting a string/enum field shall show a dropdown value; selecting a numeric field shall show a number input; selecting a date field shall show a date picker.

title: AND and OR Logical Operator Support
description: The rule builder shall support AND and OR logical operators between condition rows and between nested condition groups.

title: Nested Condition Groups Up to Configurable Depth
description: The rule builder shall support nested condition groups. MVP shall support AND/OR nesting up to 2 levels. Deeper nesting is configurable.

example.topLevel: Employment Type = Full-Time AND Employment Status = Active
example.nestedGroup: ANY OF: State = NJ OR State = NY

title: THEN Action Builder — One or More Actions per Rule
description: The rule builder shall support one or more THEN actions per rule. Action types include: Set Eligibility, Set Coverage Effective Date, Create Exception, Require Manual Review, Trigger Notification.

actionExamples.example: Coverage Effective Date = First of Following Month
Route to Benefits Administrator for review

title: Rule Testing Capability Using Representative Employee and Enrollment Data
description: The system shall provide a rule-testing capability. Users can select a representative employee record, run the rule against that employee, and see pass/fail results per condition.

title: Rule Test Results Identify Which Conditions Passed and Failed
description: Rule test results shall identify which individual conditions passed and which failed, with actual vs. expected values for failed conditions.

title: Rule Lifecycle States: Draft, In Review, Approved, Published, Retired
description: The system shall support rule lifecycle states: DRAFT → IN_REVIEW → APPROVED → PUBLISHED → RETIRED. Only PUBLISHED rules shall be used by the business rule evaluation service.
publishConstraint: Only PUBLISHED rules shall be evaluated in the production business-rule engine.

title: Rule Versioning and Version Comparison
description: The system shall maintain rule versions and provide version comparison. Modifying a PUBLISHED rule creates a new version. Version comparison shows changed fields, previous and new values, changed-by actor, and effective date.

title: Source Traceability Between Rule and Originating Document or Requirement
description: The system shall maintain source traceability between a rule and the document or requirement from which it originated. The rule UI shall display source document, page reference, and linked requirement ID.

title: AI-Generated Rules Displayed as Proposed — Require Human Review Before Publication
description: AI-generated rules shall be presented as proposed rules requiring human review and explicit approval before publication. The Rule Builder shall open pre-populated from the AI proposal with a banner indicating the AI source and confidence.

title: Canonical Machine-Readable Rule Representation Independent of UI
description: The system shall serialize rules into a canonical machine-readable JSON representation independent of the UI component implementation. The canonical representation supports Visual Builder ↔ Canonical JSON ↔ Rule Engine ↔ AI bidirectionally.

title: Only Published Rules Used by Business Rule Evaluation Service
description: Only rules in PUBLISHED status shall be used by the production business rule evaluation service. DRAFT and IN_REVIEW rules shall be visible only within the Rule Builder and shall not affect live eligibility, enrollment, or carrier processing.
